name: Team Donnelly
subject: Did you watch the debate, Peter?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
We asked grassroots supporters like you to weigh in on the most important issues facing Indiana. We were thrilled that thousands of you took the time to share with us what you're most concerned about. |
---

| | Peter, We asked grassroots supporters like you to weigh in on the most important issues facing Indiana. We were thrilled that thousands of you took the time to share with us what you're most concerned about.

Our team has been spending a lot of time over the last few weeks tabulating the results of that survey -- but we took a brief break last night to watch the first Republican primary debate. And it couldn't have been clearer that none of the three GOP candidates have demanded their campaigns to listen to Hoosier voters like Joe has.

How do we know they aren't listening? It's simple -- according to our survey results, Hoosiers' top concerns are access to affordable health care and protecting Social Security and Medicare. But last night, all three GOP candidates issued outrageous calls for deep cuts to Social Security, Medicare, and the crucial programs that hard-working Hoosiers have spent their lives paying into.

If they had their way, our health care system would be dismantled, causing average premiums for Hoosiers' to increase by $1,000 every year.

**Whichever candidate on last night 's stage wins the primary in May will have access to millions of dollars from Washington special interests, but he won't have YOU and an army of grassroots supporters on his side pitching in. Will you make a donation today to help keep Joe in the Senate and keep fighting for you?** | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_
---
**[Express donate $10>>](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2298/7377f838/301ab3cb/51fad89b/1484612305/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-debate?express_lane=true&refcode=20180221.2nd&amount=10")**
**[Express donate $25>>](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2298/7377f838/301ab3cb/51fad89a/1484612305/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-debate?express_lane=true&refcode=20180221.2nd&amount=25")**
**[Express donate $50>>](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2298/7377f838/301ab3cb/51fad895/1484612305/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-debate?express_lane=true&refcode=20180221.2nd&amount=50")**
**[Express donate $100>>](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2298/7377f838/301ab3cb/51fad894/1484612305/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-debate?express_lane=true&refcode=20180221.2nd&amount=100")**
**[Or, donate a different amount>>](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2298/7377f838/301ab3cb/51fad897/1484612305/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-debate?refcode=20180221.2nd")**
Joe's guiding principle during his time in the Senate has been listening to Hoosiers and taking their concerns to Washington. In his words, he's the hired help -- and trust me, he's heard your message loud and clear.

**But Peter -- one of the men on stage last night will have millions of special interest dollars fueling his campaign when he faces Joe in November.

We need you to step up now so Joe can keep fighting to protect health care, Social Security, and Medicare. [Will you pitch in?](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2298/7377f838/301ab3cb/51fad896/1484612305/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-debate?refcode=20180221.2nd&amount=10")**

Thanks,

Team Donnelly

Peter,

Our mission to flip the House this year begins with 11 of our women who have primaries in Texas and Illinois next month.

Want to be the first to know the outcomes of their races? Sign up for text alerts, and you'll hear it from me first with updates right at your fingertips.

**Be the first to know when our women win next month, the month after that, and in November by texting JOIN to 47717.**

Or, you can also sign up here to get text alerts:

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Be-The-First-To-Know](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c5744/24c69fd9/114e003f3/18977f4a/4081204779/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/s/20180124_website_homepage?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgBeTheFirstToKnow&utm_campaign=WQENEN43N_digital_email_20180221_SMSKnowWhoWins_inact&source=WQENEN43N_digital_email_20180221_SMSKnowWhoWins_inact")**

Meg DiMartino

name: The White House
subject: Hearing every voice
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9362fbec431f17db908965195ac392c685496ede21d98400c1c1f4641bf8408bf2eb407e54dec818d5350d3f32a89ceb19542da3b572c63a

Hearing every voice

President Donald J. Trump, Vice President Mike Pence, and Secretary of Education Betsy DeVos sat down yesterday afternoon with a large group of students, teachers, and community leaders to discuss how America must improve safety at schools across the country.

The meeting drew numerous participants from the Parkland, Florida, community. For much of the afternoon, students led the discussion. Fifteen-year-old Justin Gruber reminded attendees that 19 years ago, one of America’s most high-profile mass school shootings happened at Columbine High School. “I was born into a world where I never got to experience safety and peace,” he said.

That needs to change. President Trump has pledged more than just action—he has committed his Administration to finding real, workable solutions to end the violence.
“It’s not going to be talk like it has been in the past,” the President told students and families yesterday. “It’s been going on too long. . . . We’re going to get it done.”

That starts with making sure that every voice is heard.

Learn more:
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9362fbec431f17db2a0b8d0b0dfad5972467d21114a4d5258958f73968be38083812d3c55b6f89f977dd2e8232761d8801e66f875bf476a4
After Parkland, President Trump demands that we get it right.

‘One of the towering figures of the last 100 years’

The Reverend Billy Graham, one of the most influential spiritual leaders in modern American history, passed away yesterday at his home in Montreat, North Carolina. He was 99.

The President and First Lady felt Graham’s passing as a personal loss. “Melania and I were privileged to get to know Reverend Graham and his extraordinary family over the last several years,” President Trump said in a statement. “We are deeply grateful for their love and support.”

Billy Graham changed America for the better. “In the wake of the September 11th attacks in 2001, America turned to Billy Graham at the National Cathedral, who told us, ‘God can be trusted, even when life seems at its darkest,’” President Trump’s statement said. “We are thinking of him today, finally at home in Heaven.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9362fbec431f17db407414b4ed743b9594128f18fa3af2a6ef66752566d6c5a86f36726d4d89db569529df32e934420ff40b572c8e3b2db7
Read President Trump’s full statement on Reverend Graham’s passing.

The Moon, Mars, and worlds beyond

Last fall, President Trump told the Nation that to win the next frontier, America would once again look to send human beings to the Moon—and beyond.

Yesterday, Vice President Pence led the second meeting of the National Space Council at the Kennedy Space Center at Cape Canaveral, Florida. The Space Council had “laid dormant for nearly a quarter-century” before President Trump revived it last year, Vice President Pence noted during his remarks.

“The record is clear: Under President Donald Trump, America is leading in space once again,” the Vice President said.

That leadership is continuing forward on several fronts. Vice President Pence will deliver four recommendations to President Trump to reform the commercial space regulatory environment, including transforming the launch and re-entry licensing regime.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9362fbec431f17db477753b64e04ebd5ef4577bd39879af060417a055f83b94b9627477baac178cc1418c5f85ef0eb8d49edf1ec458b9c32
Watch Vice President Pence address the second meeting of the Space Council.

Bonus read:
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9362fbec431f17dbf4e21069fd13c81611688f0bcb5de2cec73a87af42856ed55f327f2cd97e047ed9635fcd68dface4318d2529e1f65594
Learn how the Trump Administration plans to win the “next frontier.”

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump hosts a listening session with high school students and teachers | February 21, 2018 (Official White House Photo by Shealah Craighead)

POTUS TODAY

This morning, President Trump and Vice President Pence will meet with state and local officials about school safety.

The Vice President is also delivering remarks at the Conservative Political Action Conference.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9362fbec431f17db981360c6ea90aababba8b4fe4295356ba4748ad668ace3f3c5221c1fca1c072f6b4474072dee854ff235f5e7fbe33ca9

name: Ronnie Mosley via Democrats.org
subject: This is how we prevent gun violence:
body:
I became a student activist after my friend, Blair Holt, was shot and killed on a city bus right after school.
---
Derek --

My name is Ronnie Mosley, and I lost my cousin and my best friend to gun violence.

No one is ever the same after losing a loved one to gun violence. It's why I became a student activist ten years ago, after my friend, Blair Holt, was shot and killed on a city bus right after school.

I was driven by the simple truth that kids should not have to live in fear of being gunned down in their neighborhoods. We must take proactive steps to organize in our communities and demand accountability from our elected officials.

There is still much that needs to be done -- and our best shot at reducing gun violence is electing Democratic candidates up and down the ballot who will help strengthen our gun laws.

**The time to act is now. Make a $3 contribution to throw out Republicans who have blocked progress on gun violence and elect Democrats who will take this issue seriously.**

**[ DONATE: $3](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052203e/4067b0a/5631d0135/7e558f3e/3445870750/VEsEAA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/never-again?amount=3&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=25+-+DONATE+3&utm_campaign=em_20180222_rm_s1&source=em_20180222_rm_s1&refcode=em_20180222_rm_s1")
[DONATE: $10](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052203e/4067b0a/5631d0135/7e558f3f/3445870750/VEsEAw/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/never-again?amount=10&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=26+-+DONATE+10&utm_campaign=em_20180222_rm_s1&source=em_20180222_rm_s1&refcode=em_20180222_rm_s1")
[DONATE: $25](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052203e/4067b0a/5631d0135/7e558f3c/3445870750/VEsEAg/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/never-again?amount=25&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=27+-+DONATE+25&utm_campaign=em_20180222_rm_s1&source=em_20180222_rm_s1&refcode=em_20180222_rm_s1")
[DONATE: $50](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052203e/4067b0a/5631d0135/7e558f3d/3445870750/VEsEDQ/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/never-again?amount=50&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=28+-+DONATE+50&utm_campaign=em_20180222_rm_s1&source=em_20180222_rm_s1&refcode=em_20180222_rm_s1")
[DONATE: $100](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052203e/4067b0a/5631d0135/7e558f3a/3445870750/VEsEDA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/never-again?amount=100&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=29+-+DONATE+100&utm_campaign=em_20180222_rm_s1&source=em_20180222_rm_s1&refcode=em_20180222_rm_s1")
[Or donate another amount.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052203e/4067b0a/5631d0135/7e558f3b/3445870750/VEsFBQ/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/never-again?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=30+-+Or+donate+another+amount&utm_campaign=em_20180222_rm_s1&source=em_20180222_rm_s1&refcode=em_20180222_rm_s1")
** Progress on gun violence prevention has stalled -- but right now I am hopeful that we have reached a turning point.

Inspired by the students in Parkland, Florida, people nationwide are coming together to bring an end to gun violence. Just this week, survivors of the Parkland shooting confronted lawmakers at the Florida state legislature and made their voices heard, and thousands of people gathered to support them.

School-age kids should not have to endure the trauma of gun violence. We shouldn't have to wonder, "Will I be next?"

We're fed up. We want a future, not another funeral.

There's a solution to this problem, and it starts with introducing common-sense gun laws. That's why it's so important that we come together to elect Democrats who will put people ahead of the interests of the gun lobby.

**[Show you're in to stop the cycle of gun violence. Donate $3 today and together we will defeat Republicans who want to maintain the terrible status quo.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052203e/4067b0a/5631d0135/7e558f38/3445870750/VEsFBA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/never-again?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=31+-+Show+youre+in+to+stop+the+cycle+of+gun+v&utm_campaign=em_20180222_rm_s1&source=em_20180222_rm_s1&refcode=em_20180222_rm_s1")**

We can stop needless deaths from happening -- but only if we work together.

Thanks for joining this fight.

Ronnie Mosley

Thanks for supporting our party.

subject: I thought I saw death
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tom Wolf for Governor
body:
\-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- I was beaten bloody in Alabama. I thought I saw death. I've fought voter suppression my whole life -- I know what it looks like.

Let me be clear: _gerrymandering is voter suppression._

Republicans have rigged the map to favor themselves. They've drawn districts to shut out the voice of the people.

**[That's why I am heartened to see Governor Tom Wolf reject gerrymandered maps in Pennsylvania.](https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/m/342ecf0a/73a2bc12/3ef426e0/1fcc9596/1377382486/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZhU1ZBbEpRPT0iOiIxODMyNiIsIkpTVkZUVUZKVENVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wolf-lewis-gerrymander?utm_campaign=em18_180222_lewis_fnd_clq_act_ger_1_t1&utm_medium=email&utm_source=wolf&source=em18_180222_lewis_fnd_clq_act_ger_1_t1&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&recurring=auto&amount=25&utm_content=1+-+Thatrsquos+why+I+am+heartened+to+see+Gov")** Tom knows we cannot go backward, we must go forward in this fight. Across the country, there is a deliberate, systematic attempt to make it harder and more difficult for people with disabilities, students, seniors, minorities, poor and rural voters to participate in the democratic process.

It pains me to know that Republicans are gerrymandering maps to rob Americans of their right to vote.

These oddly shaped, winding districts are part of an old strategy to shut the American people out of the democratic process. It is a clear example of voter suppression.

In Pennsylvania, Governor Wolf fought hard to reject these unfair, undemocratic maps. It's a fight that we've fought long and hard for before -- and our fight isn't over. That's why I'm asking you to get in good trouble with us.

name: NHGOP Press & Communications
subject: Weirs Times Endorses Les Cartier in Laconia Special Election
body:
**Weirs Times Endorses Les Cartier in Laconia Special Election**
**For Immediate Release:** February 22, 2018**Contact:** Patrick Hynes, 202-321-5310 LACONIA***The _Weirs Times_ newspaper endorsed Les Cartier for State Representative today in the February 27th special election in Laconia. The paper praised Cartier’s experience as a Fire, EMS and Law Enforcement First Responder and recommends his background as the perfect qualification to help lead the fight against the state’s opioid crisis, which is especially acute in Laconia. “Cartier will bring a level of experience to the state house in Concord that fits perfectly with these unique times we are going through in both the city and state levels,” the editors write. “Les Cartier will also fight for the taxpayer and small business owner,” they conclude. “Les Cartier is the candidate who fits in perfectly with these times.”
**BACKGROUND** ###

name: Debbie Wasserman Schultz
subject: Important - they want to cut Medicare
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress
body:
|
---
| | **"Trump budget trifecta: Plan aims to gut Obamacare, roll back Medicaid expansion, cut Medicare"**
_\-- CNBC_ , 2/12/2018 Repeal the Affordable Care Act. Cut Medicare. Reduce Medicaid expansions. Or put another way: **Yank people's health care. Make life harder for seniors. Strip away the safety net for disabled individuals and their families.** Why am I not surprised about that? _Because those three items have topped the Republican agenda for years_. And now that Trump's in the White House, they think they can get what they want. Let me be clear...we will not sit silent. We have fought these proposals before and we must do it again. **As long as I have you with me, we can protect those most vulnerable among us and keep the social safety net in place.** [_**If you're with me, make your voice heard today. Reject President Trump's budget and demand a plan that puts families first.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113f8b/622b7b5b/196b66e4/307bb21e/2779973567/VEsH/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e180224a") Thank you. Debbie

body:
We wanted to let you know that Gov. John Kasich will join
Colorado's Democrat Gov. John Hickenlooper and Alaska's
Independent Gov. Bill Walker in unveiling a bipartisan healthcare
blueprint at the National Press Club.

Peter, we wanted to let you
know that Gov. John Kasich will join Colorado's Democrat Gov.
John Hickenlooper and Alaska's Independent Gov. Bill Walker in
unveiling a bipartisan healthcare blueprint at the National Press
Club in Washington, DC tomorrow (Friday).

If you'd like to watch this event
live, please tune in to C-SPAN2 or visit C-SPAN.org at 10:00AM
ET.

body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c572f/24c69fd9/113d7b766/1897717d/1739341946/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFEN44N_digital_email_20180222_LeanDem_inact&source=WQEFEN44N_digital_email_20180222_LeanDem_inact")

Peter,

_Cook Political Report_ recently moved 21 House race ratings in Democrats' favor.

Now, Democrats only need to win Republican-held seats rated as "Toss Up" and "Lean Democratic" to flip the House. Of those races, 15 have an EMILY's List candidate running.

Let's replace these vulnerable Republicans with pro-choice Democratic women.

**[Donate $3 now to help our candidates build strong campaigns to defeat Republicans and take back the House.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c572f/24c69fd9/113d7b766/18976c1f/1739341946/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180222_email_LeanDem?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+3+now+to+help+our+candidates+buil&utm_campaign=WQEFEN44N_digital_email_20180222_LeanDem_inact&source=WQEFEN44N_digital_email_20180222_LeanDem_inact")**

The path for our women to flip the House keeps getting clearer. But it's not guaranteed.

If Democrats win every Republican-held seat that _Cook_ rated as a "Toss Up" or "Lean Democratic," we would only control the House by one seat. That's right -- taking back the House could come down to a _single_ seat.

So we can't take anything for granted.

We've spent the last year recruiting top-tier candidates, and now they will need our support every step of the way to win on Election Day.

**Donate $3 now to make sure our women have the resources they need to flip the House in 2018:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Support-Women-To-Flip-The-House](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c572f/24c69fd9/113d7b766/1897717f/1739341946/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180222_email_LeanDem?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgSupportWomenToFl&utm_campaign=WQEFEN44N_digital_email_20180222_LeanDem_inact&source=WQEFEN44N_digital_email_20180222_LeanDem_inact")**

| | Friends,

It's pretty simple: A woman, her family, and her doctor should decide what's best for her health - not Washington politicians.

Yet every few months it seems, lawmakers come up with new ways to try to limit women's access to health care. Whether they're placing arbitrary restrictions on health centers like Planned Parenthood, or making claims with no basis in scientific fact, we have to raise our voices to stop their efforts.

**[We need 10,000 signatures this week to demand Congress protect women's health care. Will you click here and add your name now? ](http://act.kyrstensinema.com/page/m/595d0ef4/45aebd3c/47c5e19b/2cae80bd/181089414/VEsH/ "http://act.kyrstensinema.com/page/s/sign-on-protect-women-s-health-care?utm_medium=email&utm_source=sinema&utm_content=1+-+We+need+10000+signatures+this+week+to+de&utm_campaign=KS_PET_WL_180222_choice_s1_e1&source=KS_PET_WL_180222_choice_s1_e1")** [](http://act.kyrstensinema.com/page/m/595d0ef4/45aebd3c/47c5e19b/2cae80be/181089414/VEsE/ "http://act.kyrstensinema.com/page/s/sign-on-protect-women-s-health-care?utm_medium=email&utm_source=sinema&utm_content=2&utm_campaign=KS_PET_WL_180222_choice_s1_e1&source=KS_PET_WL_180222_choice_s1_e1") A woman's decision about her life, her family and her future is a right that no one - especially members of Congress - should interfere with. I've fought for that right my entire career, and I plan to keep up that fight in the Senate. But I need you to stand with me.

name: Larry Cohen, Our Revolution
subject: Working People's Day of Action this Saturday!
body:
#
**Stand shoulder to shoulder with workers this Saturday, February 24.**
Friends,
Next week, the Supreme Court will hear _Janus v. AFSCME Council 31_. This case is the culmination of decades of attacks on working people by corporate CEOs, the wealthiest 1%, and the politicians that do their bidding to rig the economy in their favor. The forces behind it simply do not believe that working people should have the right to effectively negotiate their standard of living or conditions at work.
Janus seeks to eliminate representation fees and weaken unions and workers' bargaining rights. Weaker unions mean a stronger voice for management, but lower pay and benefits for most of the rest of us.
[**Find the closest event to you by clicking here!**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7acdf/bed43eb/4721a169/75fef159/4263734124/VEsH/ "https://actionnetwork.org/event_campaigns/working-peoples-day-of-action")
We are fighting for decent and equitable pay, affordable health care, quality schools, vibrant communities, and a secure future for all of us. Check out the details for the event closest to you:
****[RSVP](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7acdf/bed43eb/4721a169/75fef159/4263734124/VEsE/ "https://actionnetwork.org/event_campaigns/working-peoples-day-of-action")****
In solidarity,
Larry Cohen
Board Chair
Our Revolution

name: Andrew Gillum
subject: #NeverAgain
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor
body:
[](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3189/9e00690/42a2ccb5/2403b9ba/2536307486/VEsH/ "https://andrewgillum.com/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=em18_180222_GunMar_acq_gq_act__AG&source=em18_180222_GunMar_acq_gq_act__AG")
Hello,

Thanks to the brave students from Marjory Stoneman Douglas High School, we saw what democracy looks like this week.

It was my honor to welcome these strong, courageous and passionate voices to Tallahassee and march alongside them as they fought for their classmates, teachers and school -- and listen as they spoke out to make sure that #NeverAgain is a reality.

[](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3189/9e00690/42a2ccb5/2403b9bd/2536307486/VEsE/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/add-your-name-gun-feb18?utm_campaign=em18_180222_GunMar_acq_gq_act__AG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Gillum&source=em18_180222_GunMar_acq_gq_act__AG&utm_content=2")

But Governor Rick Scott isn't listening.

Last night, _CNN_ held a town hall with students who traveled from Parkland -- and Governor Scott chose not to participate. It's simple: these students and families deserve to be heard, in-person, and it's a shame the governor turned down this opportunity to help heal our state and hear their calls for gun reform.

**[I was proud to stand with the students yesterday in Tallahassee and at the Parkland town hall, but our fight is far from over. I will not stop until we have real, common sense gun reform. If you haven't already, become a citizen co-signer of my gun plan and then share it to help spread the word.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3189/9e00690/42a2ccb5/2403b9bc/2536307486/VEsF/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/add-your-name-gun-feb18?utm_campaign=em18_180222_GunMar_acq_gq_act__AG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Gillum&source=em18_180222_GunMar_acq_gq_act__AG&utm_content=3+-+I+was+proud+to+stand+with+the+students+y")**

These are young people who faced an unimaginable tragedy and, instead of staying silent, are using their stories to make a difference. Governor Scott should have had the courage to face the victims' families and discuss solutions, but he couldn't even take the time to show up.

We can make #NeverAgain a reality for our future generations, but it's going to take elected leaders who have the courage to listen.

**[We need to keep up the pressure. Add your name and then share it to help spread the word today.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3189/9e00690/42a2ccb5/2403b9bf/2536307486/VEsC/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/add-your-name-gun-feb18?utm_campaign=em18_180222_GunMar_acq_gq_act__AG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Gillum&source=em18_180222_GunMar_acq_gq_act__AG&utm_content=4+-+We+need+to+keep+up+the+pressure+Add+your")**

Thank you,

Andrew

Paid for by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor.

body:
| [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c571b/24c69fd9/11279ca68/18976853/1264819956/VEsH/ "https://www.emilyslist.org/donate?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFCN45G_digital_email_20180222_Claire_inact&source=WQEFCN45G_digital_email_20180222_Claire_inact")
---

Peter,

When I look at Claire McCaskill's Senate race in Missouri, I get a serious case of deja vu.

TWO of the Republicans running against her have some... interesting views on women.

Current state Attorney General Josh Hawley said the sexual revolution of the 1960s is to blame for human trafficking. And newcomer Courtland Sykes called feminists "career-obsessed banshees" and "she-devils."

These two sound a lot like Todd Akin -- the guy who became infamous for saying women can't get pregnant from "legitimate rape." Akin was Claire's opponent in 2012.

Claire was able to beat Akin and his disgusting views back then because women rallied behind her, including this community.

**[Donate $3 to Claire to help her beat these Akin wannabes in Missouri-- 100% of your donation will go straight to her campaign.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c571b/24c69fd9/11279ca68/1897684c/1264819956/VEsE/ "https://emilyslist.org/donate/candidate/claire-mccaskill?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+3+to+Claire+to+help+her+beat+thes&utm_campaign=WQEFCN45G_digital_email_20180222_Claire_inact&source=WQEFCN45G_digital_email_20180222_Claire_inact")**

Here's what most people forget about Claire defeating Akin: She wasn't initially favored to win.

The political analysts said Missouri was too red for Claire to hold on to this seat.

But if there's one thing you should know about Claire McCaskill, it's this: never, EVER count her out.

She put everything she had into winning in 2012, beating Akin by more than 15 points. And we are seeing the same effort from her again this year.

Whether it's defending women and families every day in the Senate or running against Republicans whose backwards views are nauseating, Claire never stops fighting. Now it's our turn to fight for her.

**Donate $3 directly to Claire's campaign to keep her in the Senate fighting for us (and keep a Republican who's a rubber stamp for Trump's agenda out). 100% of what you give will go to Claire:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Support-Claire](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c571b/24c69fd9/11279ca68/1897684d/1264819956/VEsF/ "https://emilyslist.org/donate/candidate/claire-mccaskill?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgSupportClaire&utm_campaign=WQEFCN45G_digital_email_20180222_Claire_inact&source=WQEFCN45G_digital_email_20180222_Claire_inact")**

Thanks,

Christina Reynolds

Current state Attorney General Josh Hawley said the sexual revolution of the 1960s is to blame for human trafficking. And newcomer Courtland Sykes called feminists "career-obsessed banshees" and "she-devils." These two sound a lot like Todd Akin -- the guy who became infamous for saying women can't get pregnant from "legitimate rape." Akin was Claire's opponent in 2012. Claire was able to beat Akin and his disgusting views back then because women rallied behind her, including this community. "" Here's what most people forget about Claire defeating Akin: She wasn't initially favored to win. The political analysts said Missouri was too red for Claire to hold on to this seat.
